title: weekly_rollup
description: Return a server-generated activity summary for the trailing seven local days, optionally scoped to a project. Summary text is produced by local templating in v0.1.0 (no LLM call from inside this server); richer narrative rollups via the user's MCP client LLM are a Phase 2 enhancement.

project.description: Optional project name or alias to scope the rollup. When null or absent, the rollup covers all activity across the graph.
Canonical project name as resolved, or null when the rollup covers all activity. Echoes the input intent.

period.description: Inclusive local-date window covered by this rollup. Always seven days ending today.

start.description: First local day in the window (six days before end).

end.description: Last local day in the window (today).

summary.description: Plain-text summary generated by local templating. Designed to be quoted verbatim by skills. Never contains LLM output; v0.1.0 keeps the vendor boundary clean by emitting only template-substituted text.
examples: This week on neurodock: 2 decisions recorded, 1 blocker noted, 3 candidate next actions. Most recent decision: Ship rumination detector first (Priya, 2026-05-14).

decisions.description: Decisions recorded in the window, ordered by date descending. Capped at 50.

blockers.description: Facts whose predicate is blocked_by and which are not yet resolved_by anything, ordered by recorded_at descending. Capped at 50.

next_actions.description: Candidate next-action strings synthesised by local templating from open blockers, recently-recorded decisions lacking follow-up, and any facts tagged 'next-action'. Empty array when nothing qualifies. Heuristic; not authoritative. See ADR 0002 open question on selection logic.
examples: Follow up on decision 'Adopt SQLite + sqlite-vec' with implementation owner

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset for when this rollup was generated. Optional; callers MAY use it for caching.

PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.description: A project name was supplied but did not resolve to any entity. Returned as a structured error rather than an empty rollup so callers can correct the input.

GRAPH_UNAVAILABLE.description: The local SQLite store could not be opened or is corrupted.

TEMPLATE_RENDER_FAILED.description: Local template engine errored. Should be impossible; if it occurs, the server returns this rather than a malformed summary string.

examples.name: rollup scoped to a project with activity

output.summary: This week on neurodock: 2 decisions recorded, 1 blocker noted, 2 candidate next actions. Most recent decision: Ship rumination detector first (Priya, 2026-05-14).

decisions.name: Ship rumination detector first; defer hyperfocus and sycophancy to Phase 3

next_actions: Resolve blocker on neurodock: 'awaiting  confirmation for Phase 1 launch'
Follow up on decision 'Ship rumination detector first' with implementation owner

examples.name: unscoped rollup across the whole graph, quiet week

output.summary: This week across all projects: 0 decisions recorded, 0 blockers noted, 0 candidate next actions.

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. The seven-day window is fixed in v0.1.0; a configurable window is reserved for a future minor version via an optional 'window_days' input. Renaming or retyping any field requires a new major version with a parallel $id. A Phase 2 'narrative' output field MAY be added when LLM-generated rollups become available; the v0.1.0 'summary' field remains the local-template fallback.
compatibility.side_effects: None. Pure read with local template rendering.
compatibility.idempotency: Idempotent within the same local-clock minute. Output may change as the underlying graph or local clock advances.
compatibility.privacy: Reads the local SQLite cognitive graph and renders a local template. No LLM calls, no remote calls; the vendor boundary is preserved inside this server. Summary text may contain entity names and decision titles that are sensitive; treat as user-private.
